title: Generate optional heim-pc state only with source provenance
goal: Replace the former placeholder idea with an optional, provenance-bound host-state projection that is absent when no trustworthy scan exists and can never use null or zero values to simulate current machine knowledge.

acceptance.assertion: No current host-state file is a valid and explicitly documented state; consumers do not require placeholder files.
Every generated projection binds host identifier, generator version, scan profile, source paths or APIs, start/end timestamps, redaction policy, source digests and output digest.
The default scan reads only declared metadata surfaces and never broad-scans secret, browser-profile, keyring, private-content or agent-history paths.
Consumers distinguish current, stale, partial, unknown and invalid projections; age alone cannot establish freshness when source identity changed.
The projection cannot establish live service health, repository dirty state, task priority, PR state or backup recoverability without the respective fresh primary-source evidence.
Tests cover absent state, valid generation, interrupted scan, redaction, source drift, stale timestamp, malformed provenance and private-path exclusion.

metadata.observation: state/index.json and state/repos.json contained placeholder null/zero data without timestamps or source provenance and were removed from active truth in PR #37.
